author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Alse die ander dat sien,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Hilden sine over verwoet, te dien

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Lieten sine ende sijn gekeert

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Daer men die kerstine tormenteert;

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Ende cortelike hier nare

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Gaf god Kerstoffels sinne gare

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Ende liep met stouten sinne ter stede

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Daer men hen torment dede.

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Maer hi decte sijn anscijn

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Dat hi ontsien niene wilde sijn,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Ende scalt den juge in scarpen worde.

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Een der juge die dat horde

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Gaf hem enen slach metter hant;

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Kerstoffels ontdecte hen te hant

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Ende sprac: "En waric kersten niet,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Dat ic om Gode al verdriet

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Niet wilde gedogen, ic soude an di

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Saen hebben gewroken mi."

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Doe ward die gene sere vervart

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Ende hi liep ten coning ward,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Dien hi Kerstoffels doen alseget.

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Die coning sinen sin daer an leget

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Te pensen met gedachten al

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Hoe hi Kerstoffels ontliven sal.

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Dat wi di niet vinden conden.

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: "Dat sal niet sijn", sprac hi saen,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: "Want ic sal u niet ontgaen."

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Ende in dien wege met siere lere

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Brachti die ridders an onsen here,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Die hi saen alle dopen dede.

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Ende alsi naken gebonden der stede,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Doet hi, datmenn ten coning lede

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Achter gebonden die hande bede.

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Van siere groete heeft groet wonder

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Den coning; alte hant besonder

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Hevet hi hen om sinen name gevraget.

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Dies heeft hi hem te hant gewaget

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Ende dat hi Rebebus hiet

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Ende dat hi die name liet

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: In die vonte ende ontfinc daer

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Kerstoffels tsiere name vor waer.

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: "Wat sotter namen", die coning seit,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: "Hevestu di ane geleit

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Van Kerste die gecrucit was!

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Ende wes alse seker das,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Also hi hem selven ne conde

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Gevromen in en gene stonde,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Hi di niet gevromen ne can.

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: En du ter gode dienst sout staen,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Alse wi menegen hebben gedaen."

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Doe ginc met worden die heilige man

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Vriendelike den wiven an,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: So dat hise met siere lere

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Brachte an tgelove van onsen here.

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Alse dit quam tes conings oren,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Dedise hem beiden bringen te voren

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Ende dreigetse te pinen sere,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Oft sine daden den goden ere.

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Doe togeten si gelike in dien

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Oft si sijns willen souden plien,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: So dat si vore dafgode quamen.

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Si spraken vore tfolc at te samen

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Ten beelde van Jupiterre:

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: "Oftu best god sonder merre,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Sege ons, hoe wi nu doen selen,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Want die coning wilt ons bevelen

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Dat wi di sacrificie nu doen."

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Alse die afgod ut gaf gheen soen,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Bonden si dat beelde ende ander met

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Met haren gordel ongelet

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Hebben sise ter eerde getrect

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Ende alse in pulvere berect,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Die menich minsce quam besien,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Ende hi bekerde vele van dien.

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Daer hette coning af heeft verstaen,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Dedi allen die hoefde af slaen.

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Sente Kerstoffels hi dede

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Met ysere binden alle die lede

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Ende slaen met roeden lange ure.

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Met yserinen banden sure

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Dedine noch binden om quellen;

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Onder ene yserine cellen

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Al vol viers was hi gedaen

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Met peke gemanc, dat hi so saen

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Alte aschen ward verbrand.

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Alst was smalt die celle te hant

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Entie sant bleef al gesont

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Ende maecte den lieden gods word cont,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Daer die menege bi bekeerde.

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Des conings gramskap noch al meerde,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Ende heeft te bindene bevolen

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Enen steen van ere molen

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: An den hals den heyligen man

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Ende so sleeptemenne achter straten dan.

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Alse gedaen was dese sake,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Dedine binden an enen stake

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Eens daer si daer mede sat,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Gevel bi aventuren dat

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Die baliu was int lant

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Ende Olybrius was genant,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Neven der maget liden soude.

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Hi mercte haer scoenheid menichfoude

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Ende ward met hare minnen ontsteken,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: So dat hise emmer wilde spreken.

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Ende deetse met crachte comen

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Daer hi wilde. Alsi vernomen

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Hevet die ut nemende scone maget,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Hevet hi om haren name gevraget,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Om haer geslechte, om haer wet.

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Si andwarde al ongelet

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Dat si Margriete hadde te namen;

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Haer geslechte seitse te samen,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Dattie baliu kinde edel sere;

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Si ware kersten, seitsi mere.

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Doe aiitwarde die baliu:

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: "Dese twee saken, die du seits nu,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Sijn betamelijc ter ere,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Maer dat deerde scillet sere,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Dat ene maget so edel, so scone

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Haer laet sijn in selker hone

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Ja, alse in ontbermechede,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Sprac die baliu haer an in rade

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Vriendelike, dat si dade

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Den goden ere dat si onmare

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Hadde ende ontseit openbare.

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Doe sprac gramelike die baliu:

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: "In spare twint vort meer nu,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Dat ic hars yet genadich si,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Want si bespot, dat donket mi,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Mettien die haer raden wale;

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Die gode lachtertse altemale,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Ende si ontsiet geen tormint."

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Inden karker hise sint;

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Want hi neuwe wil visieren

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Om torment van swaerre manieren.

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Alse in dien karker quam die maget,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Ward die demsternesse sere verjaget

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Metten lichte, dat God daer sende.

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Die duvel, die om der goeder scende

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Altoes poget, die quam vor hare

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Alse een drake, om in vare

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Te bringen dat magedijn,

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Ende voer te hare ward in scijn

author: Philip Utenbroeke
text: Oft hise al verslinden woude.
